rationale: In the market-uncertainty section, the citing paper references Hoeffler (2003) when explaining how firms can research demand for really new products. The citation is linked to a specific approach (conjoint with imagined usage scenarios) that the model uses as an operational example of how uncertainty about innovation rewards can be resolved. This is more than a passing name check because it helps justify a core modeling assumption about information acquisition, even though the citing paper does not empirically extend Hoeffler's study.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Hoeffler (2003) as a methodological precedent for premarket research on really new products, using it to motivate the assumption that firms can infer whether innovation will be valued. It transfers that idea into a game-theoretic entry model rather than reusing Hoeffler's empirical design directly.
